articles.full_text:
Home |Telangana |Telangana Simmers As Heatwave Sends Temperatures Past 46 Degrees Celsius
Telangana simmers as heatwave sends temperatures past 46 degrees Celsius
Telangana is facing an intense heatwave, with several districts recording temperatures above 46°C. Kumuram Bheem Asifabad reported the highest at 46.5°C, while Hyderabad touched 42.7°C.
Hyderabad: Telangana continues to reel under a relentless spell of heatwave, with a large number of districts experiencing scorching conditions for more than a week.
Based on Telangana State Development Planning Society (TSDPS) temperature data, the maximum temperatures between 8.30 am and 4.30 pm on Sunday in multiple districts soared past the 46 degrees Celsius.
The TSDPS data indicated that Kunchavelli in Kumuram Bheem Asifabad witnessed a maximum temperature of 46.5 degrees Celsius in Telangana while Langar Houz area in core Hyderabad witnessed a scorching 42.7 degrees Celsius on Sunday.
The Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation (GHMC), including suburban and nearby districts, is also witnessing equally severe heatwave conditions. Areas in and around Yadadri-Bhuvanagiri and Rangareddy have witnessed extreme heat, as maximum temperatures ranged between 43 degrees Celsius and 45 degrees Celsius.
Maximum temperatures in Telangana: (TSDPS data from 8.30 am to 4.30 pm on Sunday) (degrees Celsius)
Telangana:
Kunchavelli, (Kumuram Bheem Asifabad): 46.5
Velganoor (Dandepally), Mancherial: 46.4
Aswaraopet (Bhadradri Kothagudem): 46.3
Eturunagaram (Mulugu): 46.3
Enkuru (Khammam): 46.3
Munugode (Nalgonda): 46.3
Eligaid (Peddapalle): 46.2
Sarvaipet (Jayashankar Bhupalpally): 45.9
Maximum temperatures under GHMC zone: (TSDPS data from 8.30 am to 4.30 pm on Sunday)

articles.title: Relief In Sight For Parched Telangana After 3-Day Intense Heatwave - Deccan Chronicle
articles.full_text:
The IMD said there would be no major change in maximum temperatures during the next three days
Hyderabad: After days of intense heat, the weather bureau indicated isolated rain and thunderstorms later this week. On Sunday, who however, multiple districts reeled under a heatwave, and Komuram Bheem Asifabad and Bhadradri Kothagudem districts were the hottest with the maximum temperature at 46.5°C and 46.4°C respectively.
Yellow warnings for thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds of 40-50 kmph were also issued for several districts, including Hyderabad and Medchal Malkajgiri.
The IMD said there would be no major change in maximum temperatures during the next three days, after which temperatures are likely to gradually fall by 2°C to 3°C.
The Telangana Development Planning Society (TGDPS) said 94 mandals across 14 districts reported heatwave conditions. Bhadradri Kothagudem accounted for 15 mandals, Khammam 12, and Mancherial 10, apart from Suryapet, Nalgonda, Peddapalli, Mulugu and Jagtial districts.
The mercury remained lodged above the 40°C mark across all GHMC circles, with the highest temperature of 42.7°C being recorded in Karwan near the Golconda tahsildar office, while Uppal, Kapra, Gajularamaram and Begumpet crossed 42°C.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued orange heatwave warnings for districts including Komaram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Karimnagar, Peddapalli, Mulugu, Khammam, Nalgonda, Suryapet, Mahbubabad, Warangal and Hananmkonda for the next three days.
Amid the high temperatures, isolated rainfall was reported in parts of south Telangana. Basheerabad in Vikarabad district received the highest rainfall at 34.8 mm, while pockets of Mahbubnagar and Jogulamba Gadwal recorded moderate showers.
